requirements.remaining_gap: The repository does not expose an ordered application-facing stream abstraction.
requirements.notes: This clause depends on a stateful stream surface that is not present in the current codebase.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no connection or send-path surface that creates streams by sending data.
requirements.notes: Stream creation is still a greenfield state-machine concern.

requirements.remaining_gap: No connection-scoped stream lifetime model exists.
requirements.notes: The repository only exposes byte-oriented parsers and frame codecs.

requirements.remaining_gap: The parser classifies initiator bits, but it does not model endpoint-driven stream creation.
requirements.notes: Endpoint-driven creation policy is absent.

requirements.remaining_gap: No scheduler or multiplexing surface models interleaved concurrent stream transmission.
requirements.notes: Concurrent stream scheduling remains unmodeled.

changes_made: Added RFC 9000 S2 requirement tags to RESET_STREAM and STOP_SENDING codec tests.
requirements.remaining_gap: RESET_STREAM and STOP_SENDING wire formats exist, but there is no stream lifecycle implementation behind them.
requirements.notes: The coverage is wire-level only; the stream-management behavior remains stateful and unmodeled.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no delivery layer that could enforce or expose cross-stream ordering semantics.
requirements.notes: Cross-stream ordering guarantees remain out of scope for the current implementation.

changes_made: Added RFC 9000 S2 requirement tags to MAX_STREAMS and STREAMS_BLOCKED codec tests and fuzz coverage.
requirements.remaining_gap: MAX_STREAMS and related frame codecs exist, but no live stream manager operates concurrent streams.
requirements.notes: The repository only proves the wire encodings, not the stream-limit enforcement path.

changes_made: Added RFC 9000 S2 requirement tags to MAX_STREAM_DATA, DATA_BLOCKED, and STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED codec tests and fuzz coverage.
requirements.remaining_gap: Flow-control-related frame codecs exist, but there is no sender-side enforcement or per-stream data accounting.
requirements.notes: The stream-data limit semantics are only visible at the codec layer here.

requirements.remaining_gap: Stream-ID classification shows unidirectional types, but no data-path enforcement restricts direction.
requirements.notes: Directionality is exposed by the parsed representation, not enforced by a send/receive state machine.

requirements.remaining_gap: Stream-ID classification shows bidirectional types, but no bidirectional send/receive surface exists.
requirements.notes: The implementation exposes the type bits but not the directional transport behavior.

changes_made: Rewrote the legacy stream-ID placeholder traits in the stream-ID test suite to canonical RFC 9000 Section 2.1 IDs.
requirements.notes: QuicStreamId and TryParseStreamIdentifier expose stream IDs as first-class values.

changes_made: Rewrote the legacy stream-ID placeholder traits in the stream-ID test suite to canonical RFC 9000 Section 2.1 IDs.
Added explicit maximum representable stream-ID coverage.
requirements.notes: Stream IDs are parsed through the shared QUIC varint helper and bounded by the 62-bit QUIC varint ceiling.

requirements.remaining_gap: No connection-scoped stream registry enforces uniqueness.
requirements.notes: Uniqueness is not modeled in the current parser-centric implementation.

changes_made: Rewrote the legacy stream-ID placeholder traits in the stream-ID test suite to canonical RFC 9000 Section 2.1 IDs.
requirements.notes: Stream IDs are encoded and parsed as QUIC variable-length integers.

requirements.remaining_gap: No stream allocation or reuse prevention surface exists.
requirements.notes: Stream reuse prevention requires a stateful registry that is not present.

changes_made: Rewrote the legacy stream-ID placeholder traits in the stream-ID test suite to canonical RFC 9000 Section 2.1 IDs.
requirements.notes: The least significant bit is exposed as initiator classification.

changes_made: Rewrote the legacy stream-ID placeholder traits in the stream-ID test suite to canonical RFC 9000 Section 2.1 IDs.
requirements.notes: Even-valued stream IDs classify as client-initiated.

changes_made: Rewrote the legacy stream-ID placeholder traits in the stream-ID test suite to canonical RFC 9000 Section 2.1 IDs.
requirements.notes: Odd-valued stream IDs classify as server-initiated.

changes_made: Rewrote the legacy stream-ID placeholder traits in the stream-ID test suite to canonical RFC 9000 Section 2.1 IDs.
requirements.notes: The second least significant bit is exposed as bidirectional versus unidirectional classification.

requirements.remaining_gap: No stream ID allocator establishes the per-type minimum starting points.
requirements.notes: The repository does not yet allocate stream IDs.

requirements.remaining_gap: No stream ID allocator enforces monotonic creation.
requirements.notes: Monotonic allocation is a stateful transport concern that is still absent.

requirements.remaining_gap: No stream manager opens lower-numbered streams when IDs arrive out of order.
requirements.notes: Out-of-order opening depends on connection state that is not implemented.

changes_made: Added RFC 9000 S2P2 requirement tags to STREAM frame parser, property, and fuzz tests.
requirements.notes: STREAM frame parsing and formatting preserve stream payload bytes.

requirements.remaining_gap: No ordered reassembly or buffering layer exists to turn parsed fields into an application delivery path.
requirements.notes: The parser exposes Stream ID and Offset fields, but no reassembly layer places data for delivery.

requirements.remaining_gap: No application-facing ordered byte-stream delivery surface exists.
requirements.notes: This is still a stateful stream-management gap.

requirements.remaining_gap: No out-of-order buffering or flow-control-bounded reassembly exists.
requirements.notes: Buffering is not modeled in the current codebase.

requirements.remaining_gap: No optional out-of-order delivery API exists.
requirements.notes: The implementation does not expose an application delivery API at all.

requirements.remaining_gap: No receive-state implementation can discard already-received data.
requirements.notes: Discard semantics are not represented in the current codebase.

requirements.remaining_gap: No retransmission/send-state surface ensures byte stability at offsets.
requirements.notes: This remains unimplemented until a full stream send state exists.

requirements.remaining_gap: No conflicting-retransmission detection or error path exists.
requirements.notes: Conflict detection requires stream-state tracking that is not present.

requirements.remaining_gap: The parser exposes stream payload as opaque bytes, but there is no higher-level stream abstraction to prove the API shape.
requirements.notes: Wire-level byte exposure is present, but the policy layer that would make the requirement fully explicit is absent.

requirements.remaining_gap: No sender-side flow-control enforcement exists.
requirements.notes: Sender-side flow-control is still a greenfield transport concern.

requirements.remaining_gap: The repository has no stream scheduling or prioritization interface.
requirements.notes: No prioritization API is implemented.

requirements.remaining_gap: The repository has no application-visible priority controls.
requirements.notes: This is absent from the current surface area.

requirements.remaining_gap: The repository has no resource allocator that consumes stream priority information.
requirements.notes: Resource allocation based on stream priority is not modeled.

requirements.remaining_gap: No application-facing stream interface exists.
requirements.notes: The current codebase stops at parser and codec helpers.

requirements.remaining_gap: No application-facing stream interface exists to constrain or subset.
requirements.notes: Protocol-specific API narrowing is not represented here.

requirements.remaining_gap: No write API or flow-credit reservation surface exists.
requirements.notes: Application write authorization is not modeled.

requirements.remaining_gap: STREAM frame FIN support exists at the codec layer, but no stream API exposes clean termination.
requirements.notes: The byte-level FIN bit is covered, but the application-facing end-of-stream behavior is not.

requirements.remaining_gap: RESET_STREAM frame support exists at the codec layer, but no stream API exposes abrupt reset semantics.
requirements.notes: The reset wire format is present; the stateful stream termination semantics are not.

requirements.remaining_gap: STREAM parsing exposes readable payload bytes, but no stream API models application reads.
requirements.notes: Readable byte delivery is only represented as parsed frame data.

requirements.remaining_gap: STOP_SENDING frame support exists at the codec layer, but no stream API models aborting reads.
requirements.notes: The abort-read wire format is implemented, not the application behavior behind it.

requirements.remaining_gap: No notification or callback surface exists for stream state changes.
requirements.notes: Stream state-change notifications remain greenfield.
